title: In-context auto-compaction (threshold-triggered summarization + compact-boundary event)
description: Keep long runs inside the context window without the coarse sprint-boundary reset. When AgenticLoopParams.compaction is set and the last response's cumulative input tokens cross the configured threshold, the loop makes one summarization call via the SAME client/model over the older messages, replaces them with a single summary message (keeping the most recent turns verbatim), emits a compact-boundary LoopEvent, and continues the run. Absent config → never compacts, byte-identical.

successCriteria.description: When cumulative input tokens exceed compaction.maxContextTokens, messages older than the configured keepRecentTurns are replaced by exactly one summary message produced via a client.chat summarization call, and the most recent turns survive verbatim (deep-equal).
A compact-boundary LoopEvent fires with { messagesBefore, messagesAfter, inputTokensAtTrigger } when onEvent is present, and compaction still works when onEvent is absent.
The summarization call's usage and costUsd are charged to the run's Budget (when present) and included in AgenticLoopResult.usage/costUsd totals.
A failed summarization call fails open: the loop logs, skips compaction for that turn, and continues uncompacted (never throws, never drops messages without a summary).
Without compaction config, no summarization call is ever made and behavior is byte-identical (full existing suite unchanged).
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass.

nonGoals: Do not replace or modify the existing sprint-boundary compaction (summarizeOlderSprints / contextReset) — this is a different, in-run layer.
Do not compact the system prompt or the pending tool-result exchange (only completed older turns).
Do not add per-provider token counting — the trigger uses the usage.inputTokens the adapters already report.
Do not enable compaction for any role by default.
stopConditions: npm run build passes and tests assert: threshold-triggered replacement with recent turns preserved, event emission, budget/cost accounting of the extra call, fail-open on summarizer error, and the never-compacts absence path.
definitionOfDone: A loop given compaction config survives past its token threshold by summarizing older turns in-run via one extra model call, preserving recent turns, accounting the extra call's cost, and emitting a compact-boundary event — while a loop without the config never changes behavior. Build and full suite green.
assumptions: usage.inputTokens on each ChatResponse reflects the prompt size the provider saw and is the trigger signal (no independent tokenizer needed).
Sprint 5's LoopEvent union reserved 'compact-boundary'; this sprint implements it (hard dependsOn).
The summary message is a plain user-role text Message ('[Conversation summary] ...') in the own Message union so every provider accepts it.
keepRecentTurns defaults to 2 exchanges when unset; the pending turn currently being answered is never compacted away.
outOfScope: CLAUDE.md-style summarization steering (could be a later compaction.instructions field).
Persisting pre-compaction transcripts (sprint 6 sessions already capture per-turn state when enabled).
generatorNotes: Extend AgenticLoopParams with compaction?: { maxContextTokens: number; keepRecentTurns?: number; instructions?: string }. Implementation point: after each turn's usage accumulation (and budget charge if sprint 3 landed), if compaction is set and response.usage.inputTokens > maxContextTokens, split messages into [head ... | tail = last keepRecentTurns*2 messages + any in-flight tool exchange]; call client.chat once with a summarization system prompt ('Summarize this conversation preserving: task objective, file paths touched, decisions made, errors seen' + optional instructions) over the head; on success replace head with one summary Message, emit compact-boundary event, charge the call's usage/costUsd to the same accumulators/Budget; on failure log via existing logger and continue uncompacted. Guard against thrash: after a compaction, do not re-trigger until inputTokens again exceeds the threshold (the shrunken prompt naturally resets this). Keep ALL logic inside agentic-loop.ts or a sibling compaction.ts pure helper (summarizeMessages(client, model, messages, instructions) → Promise<Message | undefined>) for testability.
evaluatorNotes: Script a fake client whose responses report escalating inputTokens crossing the threshold on turn 3, and whose summarization call (recognize by system prompt) returns a fixed summary. sc-7-1: after the run, inspect messages sent on turn 4 — exactly one summary message where the head was, tail turns deep-equal. sc-7-2: collect events with onEvent; assert payload fields; re-run without onEvent asserting compaction still happened (message inspection). sc-7-3: Budget spy — chargeTokens/chargeUsd called for the summarizer call; result totals include it. sc-7-4: summarizer call rejects → run completes, messages uncompacted, no throw. sc-7-5: no compaction config → assert client.chat call count has no extra summarizer call and FULL suite passes. Build + typecheck.
